featureName: Distributed Tool Briefs and Recommendation History

completedPhaseClaims.summary: Scope 01 MarketSessionEvidence foundation DoD met via fresh current-certification window SCOPE01-CERT-20260719T042504Z: TP-01-01..10 controlled-mutation RED-before-GREEN (exact declared discriminators), TP-01-11 baseline node scripts/selftest.mjs = 558 passed / 0 failed, dual-runtime + Consumer/Shared-Infrastructure sweeps, validate-node-source-lock PASS, artifact-lint PASSED, final byte-identity audit clean. Committed production/test/fixture files byte-identical to HEAD. Certification pending bubbles.validate; certification.* and feature status unchanged.

executionHistory.summary: Authored the greenfield registry-driven tool briefing and recommendation-history specification with 6 actors, 8 use cases, 18 BDD scenarios, 91 requirements, 6 grounded improvement proposals, deterministic run and recommendation identity, truthful heterogeneous tool profiles, monthly agent-readable history partitions, atomic publication, migration, cost, privacy, UI, and auto-discovery contracts. Analyst checks passed; universal artifact lint remains blocked by intentionally absent foreign-owned design, scope, report, and user-validation artifacts.

Created the complete plan-owned packet with five sequential foundation-first scopes, 15 stable Gherkin scenario contracts, synchronized machine-readable test planning, independent shared-infrastructure canaries, exact persistent regression paths and titles, dirty-tree and Spec 001 boundaries, and explicit implementation routing. No product behavior or certification was claimed.

Reconciled Feature 002 in place for source-aware market-session and released-report evidence: corrected the current registry truth to 18 tools and 5 ToolModelRead publishers; strengthened the Outcome Contract and capability model; appended 3 use cases, 41 requirements (FR-092 through FR-132), 12 business scenarios (BS-002-019 through BS-002-030), 13 acceptance mappings, 9 NFRs, 2 grounded improvement proposals, and explicit requirement traceability. Artifact lint passed. Foreign-owned UX, design, scope, scenario-manifest, test-plan, report, and user-validation surfaces remain unchanged and require owner reconciliation before implementation.

Reconciled only Feature 002's UX-owned spec sections for MarketSessionEvidence. Added five registry-driven Tool Brief, Final Brief, Power evidence, and focused-history screen contracts; fourteen shared UI primitives; explicit session, report, reaction, comparability, loading, empty, stale, and error language; responsive, keyboard, screen-reader, tooltip, ticker-link, and escaped-text rules; and five journeys mapped to BS-002-019 through BS-002-030 plus the existing brief/history scenarios. No design, scope, product, test, report, user-validation, scenario-manifest, test-plan, status, or certification content changed.

Reconciled only Feature 002's analyst-owned business content for the protected live registry change: recorded the observed 18-entry registry as Market Brief plus 17 source tools while making frozen-registry discovery, not a fixed count, authoritative; replaced shorthand profile examples with a complete non-normative 18-ID snapshot; added Bond Regime data, owning-model, normalized-read, brief, history, source-rights, and honest no-action boundaries; and corrected the stale four-publisher claim to five. The existing 6 actors, 11 use cases, 30 business scenarios, and 33 acceptance criteria remained registry-generic and required no cardinality edits. Artifact lint, semantic analyst checks, and state-boundary assertions passed. No UX, design, scope, manifest, test-plan, report, user-validation, source, certification, Spec 001, BUG-001, Market Brief payload, or Bond Regime artifact was edited.

Reconciled the active contract-grade design for FR-092 through FR-132 and BS-002-019 through BS-002-030 without changing foreign-owned planning or delivery artifacts. Preserved the observed 18-participant/17-source registry, five browser publishers, and four headless publishers as canaries while making frozen-registry counts authoritative; corrected cross-date/DST comparison signatures; pinned dependency-free module ownership, exact request and source-use policies, pre-release consensus lock lineage, cutoff-safe report reactions, bounded final-author compaction, shared Simple/Power/history behavior, failure semantics, rollout, rollback, tests, and requirement traceability. Artifact lint, freshness, amended-requirement coherence, and focused design checks passed; G094 reported a grandfathered pass because createdAt is absent. Canonical traceability remained nonzero on 15 absent implementation test files in the foreign-owned pre-amendment plan, so no status, completed-phase, implementation, report, or certification claim was made.

Reconciled the amended Feature 002 plan into a ten-scope foundation-first per-directory DAG with 28 stable Gherkin contracts, 122 exact Test Plan row-to-DoD contracts, provider and owner boundaries, cutoff-safe market-session/report evidence, registry-derived 18/17 canaries, bounded authorship/history/final/publication/UI slices, and explicit human journeys. The previous five-scope plan is mechanically marked historical and its machine rows are doNotExecute. Planning checks are recorded separately; no product behavior, user acceptance execution, status promotion, completed phase, audit, or certification claim was made.

Repaired plan-owned findings F002-SR-003 through F002-SR-007 as one synchronized planning transaction: isolated the top-level legacy pointer from the active ten-scope DAG; made Scope 01 the sole owner of provider-neutral report/reaction primitives while Scopes 03 and 04 own concrete vertical integration; replaced Scope 08's runtime literal barrier with frozen-registry ID-set equality; assigned NFR-023 and NFR-024 through the active index and aggregate report; and synchronized Scope 10 canonical Playwright commands, positional TP items, all 122 LF-based row hashes, and 28 scenario links. Focused lint, artifact freshness, and deterministic plan checks passed. Installed DAG and traceability parser incompatibilities were routed through project-owned framework proposals. Status remained not_started; no product implementation, product test execution, scope completion, user acceptance, audit, or certification was claimed.

statusDiscipline.scopePickupRule: All dependsOn scopes must be Done. Pick the lowest-numbered eligible scope.
statusDiscipline.specDoneRequires: All scopes done. certification.completedScopes contains all scope IDs and bubbles.validate has certified promotion.

notes: Planning reconciled to the amended ten-scope per-directory DAG. Scope 01 is the next eligible implementation scope. Product implementation, delivery evidence, user acceptance execution, and certification have not started.
